user_input: My dog is limping and won't put weight on its leg
vet_response: Possible injury. Limit movement and avoid walking muna. Bring your dog to the clinic ASAP for X-ray.
recommended_treatment: Immobilization, pain relief meds, possible surgery

user_input: My cat fell from a high place and is not moving much
vet_response: This is serious. Keep your cat warm and still. Dalhin agad sa vet for emergency check.
recommended_treatment: Emergency care, imaging, surgery if needed

user_input: My bird broke its wing and cannot fly
vet_response: Do not force movement. I-place muna sa cage to prevent stress. Visit vet immediately.

user_input: My fish keeps flipping upside down
vet_response: Check water quality agad. Possible swim bladder issue. Change water and observe.

user_input: My dog is vomiting and has diarrhea
vet_response: Possible infection or food poisoning. Stop feeding muna for a few hours and provide water. Vet visit recommended.

user_input: My cat has not eaten for two days
vet_response: This is dangerous for cats. Kailangan na ng vet check immediately.

user_input: My dog keeps scratching and losing fur
vet_response: Possible allergy or parasites. Check for fleas/ticks. Vet consultation advised.

user_input: My cat has discharge coming from its eyes
vet_response: Clean gently with saline. If tuloy-tuloy, consult vet.

user_input: My bird is breathing with its beak open
vet_response: This is emergency. Birds should not breathe like this. Dalhin agad sa vet.

user_input: My dog ate chocolate
vet_response: Chocolate is toxic. Go to vet immediately. Do not wait.

user_input: My cat is having difficulty urinating
vet_response: Emergency ito. Possible blockage. Bring to vet ASAP.

user_input: My dog keeps shaking its head and scratching ears
vet_response: Possible ear infection. Avoid cleaning deeply. Vet check recommended.

user_input: My rabbit is not eating and looks weak
vet_response: Rabbits must eat continuously. This is urgent. Vet visit needed.

user_input: My fish has white spots on its body
vet_response: Common ito sa ich disease. Isolate fish and treat water.

user_input: My dog feels very hot and weak
vet_response: Check temperature if possible. Bring to vet immediately.

user_input: My cat is sneezing a lot
vet_response: Monitor muna. If may discharge or worsening, consult vet.

user_input: My dog has a bleeding wound
vet_response: Apply pressure to stop bleeding. Dalhin agad sa clinic.

user_input: My cat is hiding and not socializing
vet_response: Observe muna. Could be stress or illness. If persists, consult vet.

user_input: My bird is not eating seeds anymore
vet_response: Try soft food muna. If tuloy-tuloy, vet check needed.

user_input: My dog is having seizures
vet_response: Emergency ito. Keep dog safe from injury and go to vet immediately.

user_input: My dog was hit by a car
vet_response: This is a life-threatening emergency. Do not move your dog unnecessarily. Keep them warm and still. Rush to the nearest vet clinic immediately.

user_input: My cat is bleeding from the mouth
vet_response: This could indicate internal injury, broken tooth, or ingested sharp object. Keep your cat calm and go to the vet immediately.

user_input: My dog swallowed something sharp
vet_response: Do NOT induce vomiting. Sharp objects can cause more damage coming back up. Go to the vet immediately for X-ray.

user_input: My rabbit is not moving at all
vet_response: This is very serious. Rabbits hide illness well. Keep warm and rush to vet immediately.
possible_diagnosis: Shock, GI stasis, or organ failure

user_input: My hamster is cold and unresponsive
vet_response: Your hamster may be in torpor (hibernation-like state) or critically ill. Warm slowly with your hands. If no response in 30 min, see vet.

user_input: My dog keeps barking at night
vet_response: Night barking can be due to anxiety, boredom, or hearing noises. Ensure exercise during the day and a comfortable sleeping area.

user_input: My cat suddenly became aggressive
vet_response: Sudden aggression may indicate pain, stress, or territorial issues. Avoid punishment. Observe for other symptoms and consult vet.

user_input: My dog is not eating but still active
vet_response: If your dog is active but not eating, it may be picky or have mild stomach upset. Monitor for 24 hours. Offer bland food.

user_input: My pet is hiding all the time
vet_response: Hiding behavior can indicate stress, fear, pain, or illness. Create a safe space and monitor. If it persists, consult your vet.

user_input: My dog is chewing everything
vet_response: Chewing is normal in puppies (teething) but in adults may indicate boredom, anxiety, or dental issues. Provide chew toys and exercise.

user_input: My cat is not using the litter box
vet_response: Litter box avoidance may be due to UTI, stress, dirty litter, or pain. Rule out medical causes first, then check environment.

user_input: My bird is not flying
vet_response: A bird that cannot fly may have a wing injury, illness, or weakness. Keep in a safe cage and consult an avian vet.

user_input: My bird is losing feathers
vet_response: Feather loss can be molting (normal) or stress/illness-related. Check for bald patches and skin irritation. Consult avian vet.

user_input: My bird is not eating
vet_response: Birds have fast metabolisms and not eating is dangerous. Offer soft food and consult an avian vet immediately.

user_input: My bird has breathing problems
vet_response: Open-beak breathing in birds is a SEVERE emergency. Birds hide illness until critical. Rush to avian vet NOW.
possible_diagnosis: Respiratory infection or air sac disease

user_input: My fish is staying at the bottom
vet_response: Bottom-sitting may indicate stress, illness, or poor water quality. Test water parameters immediately.

user_input: My fish has white spots
vet_response: White spots are commonly Ichthyophthirius (Ich disease). Isolate affected fish and treat the tank with aquarium salt or medication.

user_input: My fish is not moving
vet_response: A fish that is not moving may be critically ill. Check water temperature, pH, and ammonia levels immediately.

user_input: My fish is gasping at the surface
vet_response: Gasping at the surface indicates low oxygen or ammonia poisoning. Do an immediate partial water change and add an air pump.

user_input: My dog has ticks
vet_response: Remove visible ticks carefully with tweezers (grasp close to skin). Use vet-approved tick prevention product. Check for tick-borne diseases.

user_input: My dog has swollen eyes
vet_response: Swollen eyes could be an allergic reaction, infection, or injury. Clean gently. If swelling worsens, visit the vet.

user_input: My cat has hair loss
vet_response: Hair loss in cats can be from over-grooming (stress), allergies, ringworm, or parasites. Check for patterns and see vet.

user_input: My cat is vomiting hairballs
vet_response: Occasional hairballs are normal for cats. If frequent, try a hairball-prevention diet and regular brushing. See vet if daily.

user_input: My cat is breathing fast
vet_response: Rapid breathing in cats is concerning. Could be respiratory infection, heart disease, or pain. Keep calm and see vet today.

user_input: My cat has fleas
vet_response: Fleas cause itching and can transmit diseases. Use vet-approved flea treatment on ALL pets. Wash bedding in hot water.

user_input: When should my pet get vaccinated?
vet_response: Puppies/kittens start vaccines at 6-8 weeks. Adult pets need annual boosters. Ask your vet for a personalized schedule.

user_input: What vaccines does my dog need?
vet_response: Core vaccines for dogs: Rabies, Distemper, Parvovirus, Hepatitis. Non-core: Bordetella, Leptospirosis. Your vet will advise based on lifestyle.

user_input: Is my pet due for vaccination?
vet_response: Check your pet's vaccination card or ask your vet. Generally, vaccines are given annually or every 3 years depending on type.

user_input: How often should I deworm my pet?
vet_response: Puppies/kittens: every 2-4 weeks until 12 weeks, then monthly until 6 months. Adults: every 3-6 months.

user_input: Do indoor cats need vaccines?
vet_response: Yes! Indoor cats still need core vaccines (Rabies, FVRCP). They can be exposed through windows, visitors, or escaped moments.

user_input: What should I feed my dog?
vet_response: Feed age-appropriate commercial dog food. Puppies need puppy formula, adults need adult food. Avoid table scraps and toxic foods.

user_input: Can my cat eat human food?
vet_response: Most human food is not suitable for cats. Avoid onions, garlic, chocolate, grapes, and dairy. Plain cooked chicken or fish is okay in small amounts.

user_input: Is chocolate dangerous for dogs?
vet_response: YES. Chocolate is TOXIC to dogs. Dark chocolate is most dangerous. If ingested, go to the vet IMMEDIATELY. Do not wait for symptoms.
recommended_treatment: Induced vomiting, activated charcoal, emergency care

user_input: What food is safe for birds?
vet_response: Safe foods: seeds, pellets, fruits (apple, banana), vegetables (carrot, broccoli). Avoid avocado, chocolate, caffeine, and alcohol.

user_input: How often should I feed my pet?
vet_response: Puppies: 3-4 times/day. Adult dogs: 2 times/day. Kittens: 3-4 times/day. Adult cats: 2 times/day. Always provide fresh water.

user_input: Is my dog pregnant?
vet_response: Signs of pregnancy: enlarged belly, increased appetite, nesting behavior. A vet can confirm via ultrasound after 25 days.

user_input: How long is pet pregnancy?
vet_response: Dogs: approximately 63 days (9 weeks). Cats: approximately 63-65 days. Rabbits: 28-33 days.

user_input: My pet is in heat, what should I do?
vet_response: Keep your pet indoors and away from other animals. Use pet diapers if needed. Consider spaying/neutering to prevent unwanted pregnancies.

user_input: When should I spay or neuter my pet?
vet_response: Generally recommended at 6 months of age. Discuss timing with your vet based on breed and health status.

user_input: Can I reschedule my appointment?
vet_response: Yes! Log in to your VetSync account, go to Dashboard, and click 'Manage Appointments' to reschedule or cancel.

user_input: How much is a consultation?
vet_response: Consultation fees vary by service. Please contact our clinic at (02) 8123-4567 or check the booking page for current pricing.

user_input: Is the clinic open today?
vet_response: VetSync Clinic is open Monday-Saturday, 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM. Closed on Sundays and holidays. For emergencies: (02) 8123-4567.

user_input: What medicine can I give my dog?
vet_response: NEVER give human medication to pets without vet approval. Paracetamol and ibuprofen are TOXIC to pets. Always consult your vet first.

user_input: Can I give human medicine to pets?
vet_response: NO. Most human medications are dangerous or fatal to pets. Paracetamol, ibuprofen, and aspirin can be toxic. Always consult your vet.

user_input: How do I treat wounds at home?
vet_response: For minor wounds: clean with saline or clean water, apply pet-safe antiseptic, and bandage loosely. For deep wounds, see vet immediately.
recommended_treatment: Cleaning, antiseptic, vet for serious wounds

user_input: What should I do for infections?
vet_response: Signs of infection: redness, swelling, discharge, warmth, or bad smell. Do not self-treat with antibiotics. Visit your vet for proper diagnosis.

user_input: Is this medication safe for my pet?
vet_response: Always verify medications with your vet. Even 'pet-safe' medications can have wrong dosages. When in doubt, call your vet.

user_input: My dog can't stand up suddenly
vet_response: This could indicate spinal injury, poisoning, or severe weakness. Do not force movement. Support with a towel sling and rush to vet.

user_input: My fish flips upside down
vet_response: Upside-down swimming is a classic sign of swim bladder disease. Fast the fish for 24-48 hours, then feed peeled peas. Check water quality.

user_input: My pet is losing weight
vet_response: Unexplained weight loss can indicate parasites, diabetes, kidney disease, or cancer. Monitor food intake and see vet for tests.
